original: runParallelBatch({ stories, ctx, prd }) returns ParallelBatchResult where completed contains stories whose pipeline passed and were merged to base branch
refined: runParallelBatch({ stories, ctx, prd }) returns ParallelBatchResult where completed contains stories whose pipeline passed and were merged to base branch

original: runParallelBatch returns ParallelBatchResult where failed contains { story, pipelineResult: PipelineRunResult } for each story whose pipeline did not pass
refined: runParallelBatch returns ParallelBatchResult where failed contains { story, pipelineResult: PipelineRunResult } for each story whose pipeline did not pass

original: runParallelBatch returns ParallelBatchResult where mergeConflicts contains { story, rectified: boolean, cost: number } for each story that had a merge conflict
refined: runParallelBatch returns ParallelBatchResult where mergeConflicts contains { story, rectified: boolean, cost: number } for each story that had a merge conflict

original: runParallelBatch returns storyCosts Map where storyCosts.get(story.id) equals the per-story cost from executeParallelBatch's storyCosts, not an even-split formula
refined: runParallelBatch returns storyCosts Map where storyCosts.get(story.id) equals the per-story cost from executeParallelBatch's storyCosts, not an even-split formula

original: runParallelBatch returns totalCost equal to the sum of all per-story costs in the batch
refined: runParallelBatch returns totalCost equal to the sum of all per-story costs in the batch

original: When executeParallelBatch returns a merge conflict for a story, runParallelBatch calls rectifyConflictedStory and sets rectified: true in mergeConflicts when rectification succeeds
refined: When executeParallelBatch returns a merge conflict for a story, runParallelBatch calls rectifyConflictedStory and sets rectified: true in mergeConflicts when rectification succeeds

original: When rectifyConflictedStory fails, runParallelBatch sets rectified: false in mergeConflicts for that story

refined: selectIndependentBatch(stories, maxCount) returns an empty array when stories is empty

refined: selectIndependentBatch(stories, maxCount) returns a single-element array when exactly one story has no unmet dependencies

original: selectIndependentBatch(stories, maxCount) returns at most maxCount stories even when more dependency-free stories are available
refined: selectIndependentBatch(stories, maxCount) returns at most maxCount stories even when more dependency-free stories are available

refined: When ctx.parallelCount > 0 and selectIndependentBatch returns more than 1 story, executeUnified calls runParallelBatch instead of runIteration

original: When ctx.parallelCount > 0 but selectIndependentBatch returns exactly 1 story, executeUnified calls runIteration for that story
refined: When ctx.parallelCount > 0 but selectIndependentBatch returns exactly 1 story, executeUnified calls runIteration for that story

original: When ctx.parallelCount is undefined or 0, executeUnified always calls runIteration and never calls runParallelBatch
refined: When ctx.parallelCount is undefined or 0, executeUnified always calls runIteration and never calls runParallelBatch
